title: Third-Party Building Plan Review Renewal -- Budget Advocacy and ROI Discussion
summary: Jake Dishaw, Deputy Commissioner of Code Enforcement, presents item 29: renewal of six third-party plan review firms for building permit review. Reports dramatic improvement -- projects now assigned same day vs sitting in queue for weeks. Council requests data comparing permit volumes before/after. Budget cut from $600K to $300K last cycle; Dishaw would request $750K+ next year as firms confirmed more staff with more funding. Council advocates ROI analysis showing permit revenue per dollar invested. Discussion clarifies distinction between NYS Uniform Building Code review and planning commission/zoning processes.

speakers.name: Jake Dishaw
speakers.role: Deputy Commissioner, Code Enforcement and Zoning

political_signal.description: Jake Dishaw and council members advocate for increased third-party plan review funding, framing it as revenue-generating and development-enabling.

processing_notes: HR detection is false positive. Third-party firms function as extension of permit office staff for NYS Uniform Building Code review only. Budget was $600K, cut to $300K in FY26; Dishaw seeks $750K+ for next cycle.
Segment 10 of 14 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 4th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: SU -> Syracuse University
